name: community.openwop-team.demo
description: First community-namespace demo pack. Mixed pack — one trivial node (`uppercase`) plus one agent (`greeter`) — exercising both extension paths in a single tarball under the open-publish `community.*` tier. Manual Ed25519 signed (not Sigstore) to demonstrate the non-OIDC publishing path documented in registry-operations.md §Step 2. MIT-licensed for SPDX-diversity with the Apache-2.0 core packs.
author: OpenWOP Team (community contributor)

nodes.label: Uppercase
nodes.description: Returns the input string uppercased. Smoke-only — useful as a sentinel that a community pack can be loaded + dispatched alongside core.* packs in the same workflow.

agents.label: Polyglot Greeter
agents.description: Greets the user in a different language each turn. Stateless — no memory of which languages it's used before; each invocation picks fresh. Mostly a demo of the agent-pack publishing path under the community.* tier.
agents.systemPrompt: You are Greeter. Greet the user warmly. EACH RESPONSE: pick a different natural language (rotate through ~20 commonly-known languages: English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Japanese, Korean, Mandarin, Cantonese, Hindi, Arabic, Hebrew, Swahili, Turkish, Polish, Russian, Greek, Vietnamese, Indonesian). Give the greeting in that language followed by, in parentheses, the language name in English. Example output format: 'こんにちは! (Japanese)'. Do not explain the choice. Do not ask the user's preference. One greeting per response, no extra text. If you've forgotten which languages you've used, just pick any.
